test.name: First plan for q_001 plans from the persisted locality entry
test.description: No plan exists yet for q_001, but a `localities` entry (loc_001, Schuylkill County / Pennsylvania) is already present because the orchestrator ran locality-guide first. research-plan is a pure consumer: it must READ that entry and plan from its facts — citing actual record availability (probate 1811+, Catholic Diocese of Scranton post-1868, PA statewide vital registration only from 1906, browse-only coal-region films) — WITHOUT calling any place-fact/wiki survey tools (it no longer has wiki_search/wiki_place_page/place_population). It may call collections_search/volume_search/place_search to discover the specific records to plan. Tests the pure-consumer behavior after the orchestration split.

input.user_message: Create a research plan for the parentage question.

judge_context: A `localities` entry (loc_001) for Schuylkill County / Pennsylvania is ALREADY in research.json — the orchestrator ran locality-guide before research-plan. The skill should READ that entry and use its facts/quirks; it should NOT call wiki_search / wiki_place_page / place_population (research-plan no longer holds those tools — the know-how comes from the localities entry). Do NOT fail it for not surveying.

The plan must cite at least ONE fact from the localities entry — e.g., probate available 1811+ at the county Register of Wills, the Catholic Diocese of Scranton (post-1868), PA statewide vital registration only from 1906, or browse-only anthracite-region films.

research-plan MAY call collections_search / volume_search / external_links_search / place_search to discover the specific records to plan — that is its record-discovery role and is expected, not a violation.
Plan items must include rationale explaining why each record type was chosen for the parentage question — generic rationale ('more records would help') is a fail.

At least one item should target a post-1850 census (1870, 1880, or 1900) to track Patrick into adulthood; the 1880 census relationship column is the best direct parentage indicator in the available fixtures.
